	<version>0.0.1-SNAPSHOT</version>
	<name>SpringBootToConsumeODataThroughCloudSDK</name>
	<description>Sample Spring Boot App to consume OData through Cloud SDK</description>
	<url />
	<licenses>
		<license />
	</licenses>
	<developers>
		<developer />
	</developers>
	<scm>
		<connection />
		<developerConnection />
		<tag />
		<url />
	</scm>
	<properties>
		<java.version>1.8</java.version>
	</properties>
	<dependencies>
		<dependency>
			<groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
			<artifactId>spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
		</dependency>

		<dependency>
			<groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
			<artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
			<scope>test</scope>
		</dependency>
		<dependency>
			<groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
			<artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
		</dependency>
		<dependency>
			<groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
			<artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web-services</artifactId>
		</dependency>



		<dependency>
			<groupId>com.sap.cloud.sdk.datamodel</groupId>
			<artifactId>odata-core</artifactId>
			<version>4.15.0</version>
		</dependency>
		<dependency>
			<groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
			<artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
			<scope>provided</scope>
		</dependency>
		<dependency>
			<groupId>javax.inject</groupId>
			<artifactId>javax.inject</artifactId>
			<version>1</version>
			<scope>provided</scope>
		</dependency>

	</dependencies>

	<build>
		<plugins>
			<plugin>
				<groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
				<artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
			</plugin>

			<plugin>
				<groupId>com.sap.cloud.sdk.datamodel</groupId>
				<artifactId>odata-generator-maven-plugin</artifactId>
				<!-- Please use the latest version here -->
				<version>4.15.0</version>
				<executions>
					<execution>
						<id>generate-consumption</id>
						<phase>generate-sources</phase>
						<goals>
							<goal>generate</goal>
						</goals>
						<configuration>
							<inputDirectory>${project.basedir}/src/main/resources/edmx</inputDirectory>
							<outputDirectory>${project.build.sourceDirectory}</outputDirectory>
							<serviceNameMappingFile>${project.basedir}/src/main/resources/serviceNameMappings.properties</serviceNameMappingFile>
							<deleteOutputDirectory>false</deleteOutputDirectory>
							<packageName>com.sl.vdm</packageName>
							<defaultBasePath>odata/v2/</defaultBasePath>
							<compileScope>COMPILE</compileScope>
							<serviceMethodsPerEntitySet>true</serviceMethodsPerEntitySet>
							<overwriteFiles>true</overwriteFiles>
						</configuration>
					</execution>
				</executions>
			</plugin>
		</plugins>
	</build>

</project>

 

 

(In this blog we will be using OData V2 and SAP Cloud SDK version 4, if you want to consume OData V4 then the dependency version needs to be changed in the pom.xml file)

  1. Go to SAP Business Accelerator Hub to download the metadata of the OData service.
  2. Search for “business” in the search box for business partner OData service (In this blog we will be using OData V2).

 

(Please login with your personal(trial) SAP user account)

  1. Click on “Business Partner (A2X)” to navigate to business partner API.
  2. Click on “API Specification” and download the EDMX (service metadata).

 

(If you want to consume an OData service that is not available in the Business Accelerator Hub like On-promise ECC/S4HANA OData service then you can fetch the metadata of the service by using $metadata and save the content in a .edmx file{any name})

  1. Create a folder called “edmx” in the resource folder of the project and copy the downloaded/created edmx file.
  2. Also create a properties file “serviceNameMappings.properties” in the resource folder.

 

 

# API BUSINESS PARTNER
API_BUSINESS_PARTNER.className = APIBusinessPartner
API_BUSINESS_PARTNER.packageName = businesspartner

 

 

  1.  Now the folder structure in Eclipse should look like below.

  1. Now compile the project by using the “maven install” command.

  1. Once the project is compiled successfully it will generate the classes for the OData service which will be used in our business logic for integration with the OData service.
  2. Now the folder structure should look like below in Eclipse.

(Sometimes it doesn’t create the correct URL in the generated service interface (APIBusinessPartnerService,java), so please verify and replace it with the correct URL as mentioned below. You can find the correct URL when you click “Code Snippet” in the “Try Out” tab in Business Accelerator Hub)

 

 

String DEFAULT_SERVICE_PATH = "odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER";

 

 

  1. Now create a controller class (BusinessPartnerController.java) to fetch business partners.
  2. Replace the below code in the BusinessPartnerController class.

 

 

package com.sl.api;

import java.util.List;

import org.slf4j.Logger;
import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
import org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;

import com.google.gson.Gson;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.Destination;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.DestinationAccessor;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.HttpDestination;
import com.sl.vdm.namespaces.businesspartner.BusinessPartner;
import com.sl.vdm.services.DefaultAPIBusinessPartnerService;

@RestController
public class BusinessPartnerController {

	private final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(this.getClass());
	private static final String APIKEY_HEADER = "apikey";
	private static final String SANDBOX_APIKEY = "---Generated API Key---";

	@GetMapping("/GetBusinessPartners")
	public ResponseEntity<?> getBusinessPartners() {
		String DESTINATION_NAME = "MyDestination"; // in productive code, use constants file to
													// retrieve destination name
		 HttpDestination destination =
                 DestinationAccessor.getDestination(DESTINATION_NAME).asHttp();
		

		final List<BusinessPartner> businessPartners = new DefaultAPIBusinessPartnerService()
				.getAllBusinessPartner().select(BusinessPartner.BUSINESS_PARTNER, BusinessPartner.LAST_NAME,
						BusinessPartner.FIRST_NAME, BusinessPartner.CREATED_ON)
				.filter(BusinessPartner.MALE.eq(true))
				.top(200)
                .withHeader(APIKEY_HEADER, SANDBOX_APIKEY)
				.executeRequest(destination);

		return ResponseEntity.ok( new Gson().toJson(businessPartners));
	}
}

 

 

  1. Generate the API key from Business Accelerator Hub and copy it in the variable SANDBOX_APIKEY in BusinessPartnerController class.

(If the OData service is from other system like ECC or S4HANA then this step is not required, and the authentication will be set in the destination which will be automatically picked up by the SAP Cloud SDK library)

  1.  Now we need to create a destination in the environment variable to configure the OData service URL and authentication details (in this case authentication is not required).
  2. Right-click on the project and select the run configuration, select the “Environment” tab in the dialogue box.
  3. Click on “Add” to create an environment variable for the destination. Keep the name as “destinations” and value as mentioned below.

 

 

[{"name": "MyDestination", "url": "https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/"}]

 

 

<<sample destination with basic authentication>>

 

 

[{"name": "MyDestination", "url": "--OData service base URL--", "user": "---user name---", "password": "--password for the given user--" }]

 

 

(You can add multiple destinations to connect to different systems)

  1. Click on apply and run.
  2. If everything is fine, then it will be a success and show the port number for the App.
  3. Now you can test it from your browser by using the application URL

Note :- This blog is written based on my personal learning and experience, please feel free to commnet for improvement.

Happy learning!!

Best Regards,

Sudhir.

entry.title: Leveraging Key User Extensibility in BRIM - Transfer data from Billable Items to FI documents
entry.content:
Have you ever wondered how to transfer an information from your incoming billable item to an FI document in your BRIM solution?

In my blog article, I’ll will show you how to extend your BRIM solution using the Custom Fields app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public and Private Edition)!

Content:

  • The sample requirement and solution approach
  • Creation and configuration of the sample custom field
  • Conclusion
  • Appendix

The sample requirement and solution approach

In a BRIM implementation with Convergent Invoicing (CI) and Contract Accounting (FI-CA) it is a common requirement to transfer a custom information from the billable item (BIT) to the item of an FI document.

The Key User Extensibility app Custom Fields allows you to extend your BRIM solution with additional, custom fields and to copy the information from a source to a target object. When you create a custom field using the app, you choose a Business Context, which determines which object is extended. The configuration of your custom fields allows you to enable Business Scenarios, a business scenario controls the automatic transfer of data. The value in the custom field is always copied from source to target, even if the source field is empty.

The diagram below provides an overview of the active Business Contexts and Business Scenarios you need to forward an information from the billable item to the FI document.

Overview active Business Contexts and Business Scenarios

Creation and configuration of the sample custom field

The following section provides basic information on how to make a custom information from your billable item object available in the subsequent Convergent Invoicing and Contract Accounting objects and finally in your ACDOCA document in FI.

1. Launch the Custom Field app (F1481) and create a new field. Choose business context Convergent Invoicing: Billable Items. In our example, the field is labelled MTBitText, it’s of type Text with a length of 20 characters. The identifier is automatically proposed by the app.

Initial field creation

2. The detailed configuration of a custom field allows you to enable several features. In our example, we want to include the new field in the relevant Fiori apps, add it to the API for billable item creation and forward it to the billing document item (Business scenario: Billable Items to Billing Document Item).

Once you have completed the configuration, publish the new field.

The screenshots below show the sample configuration:

Enabled user interfaces

Business Scenario

SOAP API

3. Once the publishing process is complete, you will notice that your field has also been automatically created for the business context Convergent Invoicing: Billing Document Item. Don’t worry, this is the intended feature, not a bug! Since we activated the business scenario Billable Items to Billing Document Item, the app generates the custom field for the target business context as well.

Now proceed with configuring your field across the remaining objects. Start with the context Convergent Invoicing: Billing Document Item and finish with Accounting: Journal Entry Item. Use the diagram from the first chapter as a guide to identify the necessary business contexts and scenarios.

Remember to activate not only the business scenarios, but also the user interfaces. Depending on your specific requirements, you may also need to enable your custom field for OData services and Form Templates.

Remark and warning:

  • You can include additional fields to the total records transferred from FI-CA to the general ledger. To do this, you must activate the Contract Accounting Business Partner Item to Journal Entry Item or Contract Accounting General Ledger Item to Journal Entry Item business scenarios.
  • Think twice if you really need to have the information in FI! If you update totals records with additional fields, the number of totals records and journal entry items may increase considerably.

After completing all the configurations, make sure to publish your custom field for all relevant business contexts.

Business contexts

4. Now it’s time to test the configuration.

4a. Create test billable items that include a value in your custom field.
The new custom field is available on the billable item and the value is shown.

Manage Billable Items

4b. Execute the billing and invoicing process in Convergent Invoicing.

The value of your custom field is copied by the business scenario from source to target.

Manage Billing Documents

Manage Invoicing Documents

4c. Transfer the reconciliation key from Contract Accounting to FI.

As usual, you can customize the grid layouts and include your field in them.

Maintain Reconciliation Key (FPF3)

Manage Journal Entries

Conclusion

From my perspective, the key user extensibility apps offer a powerful yet user-friendly solution for custom extensions in your daily work as a BRIM consultant. Additionally, they help keep the core of your BRIM solution clean, ensuring that each change remains fully compatible with future releases.

Now it’s up to you: Explore the capabilities and features of Key User Extensibility in BRIM!

I hope you find this blog post useful and that you can benefit from this feature in your projects!

 

Appendix

Apps:

  • F1481 - Custom Fields

Documentation:

  • Documentation for F1481-Custom Fields app
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud – BRIM – Extend your system
  • SAP S/4HANA – Convergent Invoicing Extensibility (OP)
  • How to transfer existing fields in a custom include to Custom Fields app

 SAP Convergent Invoicing SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management SAP Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable

entry.title: SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Target Group

Anyone interested to know more about extending SAP S/4HANA Cloud using SAP Build

 

Background

What is Extensibility

When a customer has unique business requirements that are not fully catered by standard SAP S/4HANA then there is a need to extend the system. These extensions can be classified into 2 broad categories:

  • On-stack extensibility involves extending SAP S/4HANA directly within the core system using key user tools or ABAP.
  • Side-by-side extensibility uses SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to develop custom applications or integrations outside the core S/4HANA system, ensuring greater flexibility and upgrade safety.

What is SAP Build

SAP Build ( including SAP Build Code) is the default extension development toolset on SAP BTP.

Build Tools under low-code spectrum:

  • SAP Build Apps - Create Mobile and Web applications
  • SAP Build Process Automation - Automate business processes and workflows
  • SAP Build Work Zone - Create Digital Workspaces and business sites

Build Tools under Pro-code spectrum:

SAP Build Code - Use Business Application Studio(BAS) and Cloud Application programming Model(CAP) to create Applications. And lot more.

What challenges are we trying to address here

  • Not easy to identify the available extension options for a given S/4HANA application directly in the system
  • Not easy to determine the right extension points (eg APIs, Events) for a given S/4HANA application
  • Not an integrated experience when extending SAP S/4HANA in a side-by-side manner on BTP

 

Introduction

  • A new Extensibility wizard is available with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 that provides both on-stack and side-by-side extension options consolidated into a single wizard.
  • It provides a guided experience to kick start extension creation and enables seamless navigation from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Build.
  • The wizard understands which S/4HANA application it has been started from and accordingly filters the extensions points( like API, Events) based on the business context.

This wizard can be accessed from any extensible SAP S/4HANA app as shown below. "Create Extension" option is available under the Me Area (User menu) on the top right.

On clicking the Create Extension option, the Extensibility Wizard opens up displaying the various extension options

Note: Setup steps mentioned next are needed as a prerequisite to be able to see the "Create Extension" option

 

Setup

Summary of the setup steps:

Here is a video explaining every step in detail

SAP Help pages for the setup:

  • SAP BTP setup steps - Link
  • SAP S/4HANA setup steps - Link

 

Extension options in the Wizard ( Sep 2024 )

On-stack

Custom Field

Enables addition of new fields in desired business objects. The wizard opens the Custom Field App with the business contexts pre set.

 

Custom Logic

Enables addition of custom logic via Cloud BAdIs. Here as well the wizard opens the Custom Logic App with the business contexts pre set.

 

Side-by-Side

Custom Process

Create a Process in SAP Build Process Automation that starts based on an event from SAP S/4HANA. The business context of the starting SAP S/4HANA app is pre set in the wizard to easily identify the relevant events.

A detailed explanation of this process extension option is available in this blog from my colleague.

The extensibility wizard is expected to grow with more extension options in future.

 

Conclusion

The new SAP Build Extensibility wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a simple guided experience to create extensions both on-stack and side-by-side.

If you have access to an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system, follow the setup steps and do explore the Extensibility Wizard !

entry.title: Creating side-by-side process extensions from SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
The release of SAP Build's new extensibility wizard forSAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release marks a significant step in streamlining the creation of on-stack extensions (within SAP S/4HANA Cloud) and side-by-side extensions (in SAP BTP) to help keep the core clean. The wizard, which can be accessed directly from any business application that is enabled for key user adaptation, allows key users and even developers to easily create custom process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BTP) that can seamlessly integrate with SAP S/4HANA Cloud. In this blog, we will explore the use cases for process extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and how the new wizard is a game-changer for customers looking to enhance their business processes

Side-by-side extensions

Side-by-side extensions run outside of SAP S/4HANA Cloud (or other business application) but interact with the business applications using standard extension points like APIs, events, etc. Side-by-side extensions can technically run on any application platform, nevertheless SAP provides and recommends its dedicated platform for extension: SAP BTP.

What are process extensions

Many applications in SAP S/4HANA use SAP Business Workflow enabling preconfigured workflow scenarios to be reused in various situations. The scenarios can either be implemented without any changes or configured for your business processes by making minor adjustments. On top of that, Flexible workflows allow you to define condition-based workflows for the pre-configured approval processes. For example, the flexible workflow for purchase requisitions allows you to define one-step or multi-step approval processes for purchase requisitions according to your requirements. These are pre-delivered as part of the SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. However, your use case may go beyond a typical pre-delivered approval workflow which needs a side-by-side process extension. Here are some use case patterns which warrants a process extension in SAP BTP:

  • Processes that span across systems.
  • Processes that reach the users who are outside SAP S/4HANA.
  • Use certain capabilities from SAP BTP, like SAP AI Services.
  • Any other requirements that are not covered in the standard processes and workflows.

Example use case

Consider a specific use case where you want to make sure that every invoice created in the system has a reference to a purchase order without user intervention. There are multiple ways this can be achieved including a process extension. A side-by-side process extension in SAP Build Process Automation (in SAP BTP) can check if a purchase order is assigned for every new supplier invoice created in the system. If not, then the process shall identify the right purchase order using SAP AI Services in SAP BTP like Document Information Extraction, Data Attribute Recommendation, Invoice Object Recommendation, SAP AI Core, etc. When the machine learning model could not find the right match, then it could inform the procurement department member for further action. This process extension would run alongside the standard procurement process, adding the necessary functionality without altering the original process.

Why SAP Build Process Automation?

SAP Build Process Automation in SAP BTP with its rich development tools, integration capability and pre-built content is the recommended process extension platform for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. SAP Build Process Automation has 500+ pre-built content packages for extending SAP and non-SAP applications. Most of those content is free-to-use and can be accessed from SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition 2408 release, the extensibility wizard streamlines the creation of process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation. The process created in SAP Build Process Automation is deployed & executable in SAP BTP and can be triggered automatically when a business event is observed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

Creating a process extension

Let us see how easy it is to create a process extension with the new wizard.

Pre-requisite:

  • Setup SAP Event Mesh or SAP Advanced Event Mesh

A business event-based triggering of process is a capability of SAP Build Process Automation. It requires SAP Event Mesh or SAP Advanced Event Mesh to observe the events in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to initiate a process in SAP Build Process Automation. Refer to this blog for the detailed setup instruction.

  • You have the right business role and can access the extensibility wizard as described in previous blog of this series.

Procedure

  • From any application which is enabled for key user adaptation, open the 'Me Area' to choose the new 'Create Extension' option to launch the extensibility wizard.

Choose Process Extension. (If you do not have entitlement to SAP Build Process Automation in the connected SAP BTP tenant, then this option will be disabled with a 'Not subscribed' ribbon in the tile).

Based on the application from where the extension was triggered, the wizard automatically identifies the available extension points (in 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition, only business events are supported. More extension points is planned as future increments).

Choose Business Events, and proceed to next step.

The wizard also recognises the event objects that are relevant for the application and automatically applies a filter on the business object. Of course, you can override these filters and search any event that is needed for your use case. Select the appropriate event object and proceed to the next step.

All events of the selected event object are shown. Select the appropriate event and proceed to the next step.

Give a name and description for your extension project that will be created in SAP Build (SAP BTP). 

Confirm the changes in the Summary step or go back to edit any steps. This creates an extension project for based in the inputs given in the previous steps.

Once the project is created successfully, you can directly navigate to the project in SAP Build. 

When navigated into SAP Build (in SAP BTP), you can see a process automation project with a process created for you. 

When you open the process, you can see that the event selected in the wizard is automatically added. All the context from the selected business event is now available at your disposal. 

You can proceed to use the low-code process builder to create an extension process as needed. You can call any APIs like the SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP Cloud Integration models or any SAP BTP services, you can add a user task that creates a task in My Inbox or SAP Task Center, you can call an RPA bot automation, you can send notification emails, etc. Once the process is created, release a version and deploy the content into SAP BTP. 

When this process automation project is deployed in SAP BTP, it listens to the specific event in the connected SAP S/4HANA Cloud. When such an event is observed, it starts an instance of the process with the context that is available in the business event.

For more information on the lifecycle, refer to the help documentation of SAP Build Process Automation.

Key take aways

  • A brand-new wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is available with 2408 release to create on-stack and side-by-side extensions.
  • The wizard can be called from any application in SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition that is enabled for key user adaptation.
  • Discover the available process extension points based on the application that was used to start the wizard, through the concept of Business Context.
  • Create a side-by-side process extension in SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BTP) directly from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Look-ahead plans

  • Management of side-by-side extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  • More extension points for process extensions apart from the business event.
  • Enable the wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition.

entry.title: Responsibility Management: Creating custom teams & responsibility definitions in S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
Hi All,

As we move towards a clean core and an intelligent enterprise, we need a standard framework which can alert the user about the tasks which need their attention. With Responsibility management in S/4 HANA, SAP provides a standard set of apps which will be used to configure teams, assign members and create agent determination rules for standard as well as custom workflows.

OVERVIEW

Common Approaches:

In any S/4 HANA project, there are multiple scenarios where customers need to determine agents for approval of purchase order, invoices, sales order, billing document, Master data creation/change etc. For each of these processes we use different ways to determine agents, like BADI, BRF+ and custom tables.

Challenges:

All of these methods of determining agents depend on IT teams for configuration and coding, as well as not very user friendly for the end user to setup agent determination rules. The current approaches are not very scalable, especially with side-by-side scenarios running SAP BTP, ABAP environment 

In comes Responsibility management.... With this SAP provides a standard framework to create teams and assign members for various scenarios running either on stack or side by side on SAP BTP.

For more details, please refer to the standard documentation here

 

MAIN CONTENT

In the scenario which we will cover today, the workflow runs in SAP Build Process Automation and the teams are created in S/4 HANA and exposed via ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model API.

But before that a quick overview of the terms to be used in the blog

Team category: It is the classification of team by business processes like Finance, supply chain etc.

Team Type: Each team category can have multiple team types, it's breakdown of team by business process like, Finance team for USA and Finance team for UK.

Responsibility Attributes: The attributes based on which teams will be created and members will be assigned to the team.

There are standard team types, team category & responsibility definitions which SAP provides to cater to different scenarios, however in my case I have created custom responsibility definitions, team category and team types.

1. Create a custom team category using Manage team categories app. The team category name will be prefixed by default by identifier which is configured in ABAP transport organizer (ATO). For my case it was ZZ1.

2. Create a team type and assign it to the custom team category.

3. Create custom responsibility definition. To create custom responsibility definition, you have to create CDS views and assign them to the team category.

2. This CDS view has 2 backend tables, one for code and other for text. On top of these 2 tables, 2 CDS views are created.

4.  Function and Function profile needs to be created, this is required to assign the team members to a particular team. The functions can also be used for controlling authorizations, however in this case we are only using functions as a pre-requisite step to create members of a team.

5. Create the team using Manage team and responsibilities app. Once you select a particular team type automatically all the responsibilities assigned to the team category will automatically show up and you can provide the necessary values.

Each of the team member will be business partner. For each SU01 user a corresponding BP needs to be created, then only it will show up in this app.

6. These teams are stored in backend tables but for easy access there are standard data definitions. These can be used in your ABAP code.

7. To expose these teams to external applications which are running side-by-side your S/4 HANA core, standard API's are available. Detailed documentation can be found here.

8. In our case we have created a RAP API using custom entity, to extract the team's data along with some additional parameters and send it to SBPA via a pull mechanism.

PS: The details of RAP API are not part of this blog.

CONCLUSION:

Responsibility management provides a one stop solution for all your agent determination needs. It can help consolidate process and application specific agent determination requirements into a single application. It would help in removing data silos for agent determination rules, provides a scalable framework which can be used across SAP applications with proper authorizations.

Please free to share your thoughts and reach out to me if you have any questions.

SAP S/4HANA SAP BTP, ABAP environment SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition  ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Leveraging Key User Extensibility in BRIM – Master data objects
entry.content:
In my last blog post on Key User Extensibility in BRIM (SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management), the focus was on transactional data.

Now, I want to shift the focus to the extension of BRIM master data objects. Specially, we will take a closer look at the Contract Account and the Provider Contract Item.

Content:

  • Business Context Contract Accounting: Contract Account Partner Relationship
  • Business Context Provider Contract Item
  • Conclusion
  • Appendix

Business Context “Contract Accounting: Contract Account Partner Relationship”

This business context enables the creation of a custom field for the Contract Account Partner Relationship. Our example field MTCaExt has the type Text (10), and we have made it available in the user interfaces and SOAP APIs.

In addition, we activated the Business Scenario Contract Account Partner Relationship to Contract Accounting Business Partner Item.

The sample custom field

Since we enabled the User Interfaces, our sample field is now included in the relevant apps. It appears in the Customer Fields section on the General Data tab. You can maintain this field using both the Creation and the Change apps for the Contract Account object.

The sample field on the Contract Account object

As you already know from the previous blog, a Business Scenario enables the automatic transfer of data between objects. The diagram below illustrates the available Business Scenarios for Business Context Contract Accounting: Contract Account Partner Relationship.

Overview available Business Scenarios

When a FI-CA document is posted, the value from the Contract Account master data object is transferred to the FI-CA document and displayed in the user interface as we have activated the scenario for Business Partner Items. The business partner item view now has its own tab for Customer Data, where the field is shown.

Custom field transferred from Contract Account to BP item

The field is also available in the data structures of the Contract Account object used in the relevant Cloud BAdIs or function modules in on-premise systems. For example, consider FKK_EVENT_8135: The structure CAPARTNER – Contract Account Partner-Specific is now extended with our custom field and you can use the information in your own implementation of the BAdI.

The sample field in the import parameter of BAdI FKK_EVENT_8135

The field is now part of the service’s WSDL, as we have enabled the field for the SOAP APIs. In this example, we extended the FKCMANAGECONTRACTACCOUNT service. The response of the ContractAccountRetrieve operation now includes our custom field, YY1_MTCaExt. The create and update operations of this service have also been extended.

The sample field in the response of FKCMANAGECONTRACTACCOUNT

There are many features more to explore. Why don’t you try them out? 😊

Business Context “Provider Contract Item”

The Provider Contract Item object can be extended with custom fields by using the corresponding business context. The example field MTPcExt is of type Text(10) and enabled for SOAP API usage, in the user interfaces and APIs. As an example for automatic data transfer between objects, we activate the Provider Contract Item to Contract Accounting Business Partner Item scenario.

Enablement of SOAP APIs

All the business scenarios available for data transfer are illustrated in the graphic below. Of particular interest is the scenario Provider Contract Item to FI-CA / RAR Transfer Record, which can be highly useful in BRIM to RAR integrations.

Overview available Business Scenarios

In scenarios involving external management of the provider contract object, you may use SOAP API FKCMANAGEPROVIDERCONTRACT. This API supports the use of fields created via Custom Fields app. The screenshot below provides a schematic visualization of the ProviderContractCreate-Request.

Schematic provider contract create request with the custom field

You can now display the information in the provider contract display app by adding it to the layout of the provider contract item:

Display provider contract item with the custom field

During the posting process of a FI-CA document that references this provider contract item, the value of your custom field is transferred to the business partner item.

Custom field transferred from Provider Contract Item to BP item

You may recall that we have also activated the APIs for this custom field. We will now validate its effect on the CDS view I_ProviderContractItem using our View Browser app. The definition of this CDS view includes our example field, which you can use when consuming the view.

Definition of view I_ProviderContractItem

Conclusion

With this article and the previous one about transactional data, you should now have a solid foundation for your first steps in the area of Key User Extensibility in BRIM. We encourage you to explore the features and learn how to utilize them; you will quickly see their benefits.

Support our common mission to keeping the S/4HANA core clean! 😊

Appendix

Apps:

  • F1481 - Custom Fields
  • F2170 – View Browser

Documentation:

  • Documentation for F1481-Custom Fields app
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud – BRIM – Extend your system
  • SAP S/4HANA – Convergent Invoicing Extensibility (OP)
  • How to transfer existing fields in a custom include to Custom Fields app

entry.title: Extensibility of Thailand Withholding Tax Report - 50BIS
entry.content:
Scenario

The requirement is to display type of service for each type of tax displayed at point 5 in Thailand Withholding tax report 50BIS.

As per standard layout provided by government (50BIS_Thai (rd.go.th)), input to display such information is not present, hence report generated from DRC's standard report will also not have such details. 

However, required details (which is coming from tax code description) are exposed in service - FDP_FIWTTH_50BIS_SRV with variables named withholdingtaxcodename51, withholdingtaxcodename52 and withholdingtaxcodename53.

We can use these fields directly in mapping of adobe forms as per their requirements.

Steps

Download existing layout and change it as per requirement.

  • Open App 'Maintain Form Templates'.

Figure 1: Maintain Form Template

  • Search With 'FIWTTH_50BIS' under pre-delivered templates.

Figure 2: Search Standard Form

  • Select form and download it.

Figure 3: Download Standard Form

  • Modify form as per custom requirement (using Adobe LiveCycle Designer) to display withholding tax code descriptions for tax lines displayed at point 5.
  • Variables withholdingtaxcodename51, withholdingtaxcodename52 and withholdingtaxcodename53 are exposed which stores withholding tax code descriptions for tax items displayed in same sequence.
  • As part of this demo, we will do concatenation of all 3 values and showing it along with point5's existing description.

Upload customized Form Template

  • Open same app - 'Maintain Form Templates' and click new under Custom Templates.

Figure 4: Upload Custom Form Template

  • Provide custom form name and description. Select Data source as 'FDP_FIWTTH_50BIS_SRV', Form type as 'Content' and Available countries/regions as 'Thailand' and Upload modified form.

Figure 5: Custom Form details

Create/Extend Report Definition

  • First, we will create copy of existing standard report definition 'TH_WHT_50BIS_V1' in custom object and will make required modifications in it. You can extend existing report rather than copying and modifying.
  • Open App - 'Define Statutory Reports'.

Figure 6: Define Statutory Reports

  • Search report 'TH_WHT_50BIS_V1' and click copy, provide custom report definition name to and save.

Figure 7: Copy Existing Report

  • Click on custom report definition name to navigate inside.

Figure 8: Navigate to Custom Report Definition

  • Scroll down to Section 'Correspondence Definition'. We can see standard Correspondence with Form Template 'FIWTTH_50BIS'. We have to replace this with custom form which we created earlier. We cannot directly edit standard form details, so delete existing one and create new custom correspondence (Before deleting, save other existing details like query id, output type and parameters so that in while creating custom correspondence we can input those fields as it is).
  • Select existing correspondence and delete using button given on toolbar. 

Figure 9: Delete Existing Correspondence Definition

  • Add new custom correspondence. Provide Correspondence ID, Form Template name, which was created earlier, Query ID as 'TAX_ITEM', Correspondence Name and Output Type as 'ACCT_WHT' and click OK.

Figure 10: Add new Correspondence Definition

  • Once created, click on it to navigate inside where we need to map parameters. Map it as shown in below picture.

Figure 11: Correspondence ID Parameters

  • Save it, go back and active Report definition.
  • You can find more details about report definition of SAP Help portal - Creating a Report Definition | SAP Help Portal

Create/Extend Report Category

  • Create/Extend Reporting category to include newly created Report definition under Reporting activity. Use App 'Define Statutory Reports' for the same.
  • While creating a new report category, refer to the existing standard report category 'TH_WHT_50BIS' and activity ID 'TH_WHT_50BIS_V1'. Keep all parameter values, configurations, and settings identical to those in the standard report definition under the reporting activity, with the only change being the new report definition name for the Activity ID.
  • To add new activity, Make use of 'Assign' button.
  • For this demo, new report category is created and added newly created report definition as reporting activity, it will look like below.

Figure 12: Create Report Category

  • You can find more details about Report category of SAP Help portal - Creating a Report Category | SAP Help Portal

Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting

  • After successfully creating report category, need to configure it in reporting entity. You can create new entity or can use existing standard reporting entity used for Thailand Withholding tax reports - TH_WHT_ENT.
  • Add the newly created report category to the reporting entity and configure all relevant settings such as Periodicity, Properties, Parameters, and Organization units, as applicable. Use the standard configurations of the reporting entity 'TH_WHT_ENT' and report category ID 'TH_WHT_50BIS' as a reference. The only change will be replacing the report category name with the custom name. 
    • Public Cloud Customers can access this configuration using SSCUI - 101071.
    • Private Cloud Customers can access this configuration using transaction code SM34 => select View Cluster SRFVC_RPG_ENT.
  • For this demo, new report category is added in standard reporting entity and did all related configs, it will look like below.

Figure 13: Setting Up Statutory Reporting

  • You can find more details about Report category of SAP Help portal - Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting | SAP Help Portal

Configuration of Form Template ID

  • To Configure the Form Template ID, proceed as follows:
    • Public Cloud Customers - Open SSCUI - 102313.
    • Private Cloud Customers - In Customizing for Cross-Application Components, go to Output Control > Assign Form Template.
  • In the Assign Form Template activity, choose New Entries.
  • In the New Entries: Overview of Added Entries page, enter the field values as mentioned below.
    1. In Application Object Type, enter SRF_REPRUN.
    2. In Output Type, enter ACCT_WHT.
    3. In Form Type, choose Output Forms (Gateway interface). (If it's present).
    4. In Form Template ID, enter custom form name. 

Figure 14: Assign Form Template

Generating the Withholding Tax - 50BIS Report using Run Statutory Reports

  • Before running the reports, make sure your user has all authorization to access required business objects like company code, vendors / customers, business place etc. and to execute newly created reports.
  • Run newly generate report and verify generated output.
  • Below is output generated using standard report where tax code description is not present.

Figure 15: Output Generated with Standard Report

  • Below is output generated using custom report, as we can see tax code description (service type) is printed as per custom form layout.

Figure 16: Output Generated with Custom Report

Summary

Figure 17: Summary

entry.title: Maintain Translations for Developer Extensions in S/4HANA and BTP
entry.content:
The Maintain Translations app offers a simple way to translate developer extensions into other languages. Developer extensions refer to objects created via developer extensibility, such as Data Elements, Message Classes, Business Configuration Objects, Data Definitions, Metadata Extensions and more.

For more information about the app, check this SAP help link: SAP Help - Maintain Translations.

To learn the process for maintaining translations, visit this blog by Vijay. The process is applicable to both BTP and S/4HANA as mentioned below -

 

Additional Steps:

After following the steps in the blog, you may be prompted to enter a transport request when uploading and publishing translations. Ensure you have created a TR under your name, as it will be required during this step.

Once completed, the changes should reflect in the Fiori app when accessed in another language. However, you may encounter situations where the translations don't immediately reflect in the UI service.

Resolving Translation Reflection Issues:

After translating the CDS annotations of a metadata extension or data definition, it is essential to activate the object again in ADT. This can be done by making a trivial change, like adding and removing a space, and then reactivating it. This step must be performed after publishing the translation in the Fiori app.

Why is Activation Necessary?

Fetching the service metadata from the server involves 5 layers of caching mechanisms, at least two of which only react on (translation) changes if an internal timestamp is changed. So far only the activation of objects involved in the service stack are able to update this timestamp. Since the Maintain Translations application is external to the service stack, the combined effort required to enable a reliable automatic refresh of the translation data in the service metadata as part of the Maintain Translations publish would be quite high, hence this is still in pipeline for SAP to implement.

Due to how the translation data of metadata extensions and data definitions is handled in the transport system, this issue can only affect the development system and the quality / productive systems in which the translations are imported will always have the up-to-date service metadata.

Thanks for reading!

entry.title: How to create custom cubes, queries and measurements using SAP key user tools
entry.content:
SAP key user tools can support end user to create their own cubes, queries and custom measurements. In this blog, we use sales analytics CDS View as example.

Needed Roles:

  • SAP_BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST(Create Customizing Queries)
  • SAP_BR_SALES_MANAGER(Show sales and billing analytics data)

 

Create Custom Cube

1. Open app "Custom CDS Views" and click "Create".

 2. Input view name and select the scenario Analytical Cube.

 3. Add the primary data source, e.g., I_BillingDocumentCube

 4. If primary data source cannot cover all required value, you can add associate data source. Click "Add" -> "Associated Data Source".

 5. Select associated data source.

 6. Click the button "Join Condition" to add join condition if required.

 

7. Add join condition. E.g., Billingdocument and billingdocumentitem 

 

8. You can define Parameter Value if parameter is required. After setting is done, check the color from red to blue. Go to tap Elements, click "Add"->"Elements".

 

9. Add all the elements what we need in the report.

 10. Go to tap "Elements Properties". During searching each fields, check the selections are correct.

 

11. Go to tap "Filter", if you need some filter options, e.g., sales organization.

 

12. All are done, click "Check" and "Preview data".

 

13. Click button "Publish". Custom CDS View is completed.

 14. Click tap "Next Steps" and choose button "Custom Analytical Queries".

 

Create Custom Query based on created custom cube

15. Click "New" to create new query. (App "Custom Analytical Queries"). Add Query Name and Data Source which is created in the last, then click "OK".

 

16. Filed selection(For example, Tax Amount, Net Value, Sakes Volume Qty, Credit Memos Qty, Sales Volume, Credit Memos, Billing Document etc.,)

Create Custom Measurements

 17. In section "Display", click "Add"->"Add Converted Measure". If the measurements only have transaction currencies, this function can convert the transaction currency to a fixed currency or be an external input.

 18. Input Label and Name. Select type "Currency Conversion". Then click "OK".

 19. Select value help of Target Currency.

 20. You can select a fixed value.

 21. You can also select User Input Filters.

 22. Add Calculated Measure. This function can enable you design your own measurement using existing fields/measurements.

 23. Input Expression. For example, Amount with Tax = "Net Value" - "Tax Amount", Net Quantity = "Sales Volume Qty" - "Credit Memos Qty".

 24. In section "Filters", set some filters on dimension if necessary. Preview the content of custom analytical query by click "Preview" button, and you can check the preview data if it is as expected.

 

25. Input exchange rate type and display currency, then click Go. This query will be previewed in Web Dynpro framework.

 

26. Click back, page will be back to your custom query. Save draft and click "Publish".

 27. To save the custom analytical report to your homepage of launchpad, click "Preview" and you can click "Save as Tile".

28. Input Tile and select a Page. Then you can open this query from your launchpad.

 

Save report as a Tile

29. If you want to share the custom analytical report to others which means the report can be visible and accessed by authorized users. Open app "View Browser".

 30.Select the custom analytical query which you created, and click "Create Application". After the application is created, you can see the Application ID available to the custom analytical query.

 31. Enter Title and Subtitle, then click "OK".

 

32. Application is created.

 

33. Click the application ID  link, then a popup dialog will display. Click "View in Catalog" to navigate to app "Custom Catalog Extensions".

 

34. Add custom analytical query to business catalogs. Click "Add"

 

 35. Search the business catalog.

 36. Select the business catalogs which you want to  publish, click "Publish"

 37. When the status is published, the custom analytical report should be available to the users who have been assigned the business catalogs.

 

38. In app finder, the custom analytical report is displayed in assigned business catalogs. You can add it to pages.

 39. You can open your custom analytical report from your launchpad.

entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Build Code – September 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

As we wrap up another exciting month, we wanted to take a moment to share some of the latest updates with you. With September coming to a close, we are getting ready for two major events in the SAP Community - Devtoberfest and TechEd. For more information, be sure to read the rest of this release information.

 

Support SAP S/4HANA Cloud Service Integration for Data Model and App Logic

You can use our generative AI assistant Joule in SAP Build Code to help you to write code quickly and easily by providing natural language prompts. As you know, we are constantly listening to your feedback to make our tools even better. One request we got was to enable AI support for external service scenarios. We are excited to tell you that it is now possible to use Joule to generate code for your integrated SAP S/4HANA Cloud services.

You start by creating your full-stack application, importing an external SAP S/4HANA Cloud service, and defining the data model and services. (With Joule, you can easily create your data model and services.)

Data Model and Service creation with the help of Joule

With our new feature, it is now possible to use Joule to enhance the data model you created before: You can add associations between the external entity and local entities, and you can expose the external entity to a local service via a projection.

Using Joule to add an association between the entity Books and the external entity A_BusinessPartner

We've also added support for custom logic generation to read the associated external SAP S/4HANA Cloud entity and to manipulate the exposed external entity.

Using Joule to add a custom logic reading data from the external entity A_BusinessPartner

 

SQL Notebooks for SAP HANA

SAP Build Code offers notebook functionality, similar to the well-known Jupyter notebooks. Since Q3 2024 the SAP HANA tools in SAP Build Code offer a notebook experience for executing SQL statements on an SAP HANA Cloud database.

To get started, create an SAP HANA dev space. Inside the dev space, create a file with the .notebook extension and open it. The file will be opened with the default notebook editor. Next, create a code block. The default code block type should be “SAP HANA Connection Management”. This code block type allows opening, closing, and selecting a database connection. Typing ‘#’ opens the value help with the available commands.

Choose the “List database connections” Command

Choose the “List database connections” command and execute the code block. A list of the available database connections from the SAP HANA Database Explorer view will be shown. You can choose a database connection to use for the notebook by clicking on its name in the list. The selected database will be highlighted with a different background color.

Select Database Connection

Now you can create another code block for executing SQL statements. Be sure to change the code block type to “SAP HANA SQL” by clicking on the “SAP HANA Connection Management” indicator in the bottom right corner and choosing “SAP HANA SQL” from the subsequent dialog.

Execute SQL Queries

Add one or more SQL statements and execute the code block. The results will be shown below the code block.

Add SQL Statements and Execute the Code Block

You can add more code and markdown blocks as needed.

 

Use the Joule functionality to enhance your MDK projects in SAP Build Code

Remember back in June when we announced you could create and manage your mobile development kit (MDK) apps directly from the SAP Build lobby and continue their development using the Storyboard in SAP Build Code? We're thrilled to share another exciting update on that front!

You can now take advantage of the generative AI assistant Joule to enhance your existing MDK projects in SAP Build Code. For further details, I recommend to read this blogpost written by my colleague Jitendra.

 

Devtoberfest and TechEd

Get ready for an exciting journey of learning and collaboration with Devtoberfest and SAP TechEd Virtual!

From September 23rd to October 25th, 2024, join us for Devtoberfest, a celebration of coding and collaboration within the SAP Community. This virtual event is designed to prepare you for the highly anticipated SAP TechEd. My colleague Ravi summarizes all you need to know about SAP Build Code at Devtoberfest. For further information, read the Devtoberfest 2024 Welcome blog post and join the Devtoberfest group in SAP Community to take part in this great event!

And don't forget to mark your calendar for SAP TechEd Virtual on October 8-9, 2024, your gateway to the latest innovations and advancements in SAP technologies! The session catalog is now live, featuring a wide range of topics across various tracks, including the Application Development and Automation and the Data and Analytics tracks, where you'll find dedicated sessions on SAP Build Code. You are looking for a summary where to find SAP Build Code at SAP TechEd? Then I recommend to read this blog post.

Don't miss these opportunities to enhance your technical expertise and connect with the SAP community.

 

Let Us Hear Your Voice!

We prepared a short survey, focused on improving the product to better fit your needs. By participating, you can help us improve SAP Build Code and SAP Business Application Studio. It should only take 5 minutes to complete. Please take the survey now.

 

For further information on new features, please check our roadmaps: roadmap for SAP Build Code, SAPUI5 Road Map. And have a look at What's New in SAP Build Code, the What's New Viewer for SAPUI5 and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Release Notes. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates for SAP Build Code: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding! 😊

entry.title: SAP Developer News, September 26th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/26/sap-developer-news-september-26th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Extensibility Wizard

  • Blog by Karthik Thirunavukkarasan, overview of Extensibility Wizard: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-extensibility-wizard-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13867427
  • Blog by Seshadri Sreenivas Ramanarayanan, on side-by-side extensions with SAP Build process Automation: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/creating-side-by-side-process-extensions-from-sap-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13867657
  • Documentation:
    • Setup on BTP: https://help.sap.com/docs/build/sap-build-core/use-extensions?version=Cloud
    • Setup on S/4HANA Cloud: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b2ee5784ccfc45d5acf42d4c60eb5270.html
  • Antonio Maradiaga’s videos on setting up event mesh for SAP Build Process Automation:
    • SAP Tech Bytes: Configuring SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh
    • SAP Tech Bytes: Delivering events from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to SAP Build Process Automation via AEM
  • Devtoberfest Session: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/s-4hana-extensions-start-fast-with-the-new-extensibility-wizard/ev-p/13795541
  • Petoberfest: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-discussions/petoberfest-2024/m-p/13872559

Devtoberfest Week 1 Recap

  • Devtoberfest Homepage: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Contest Week 1: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-week-1-contest-activities/ba-p/13872705
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d

Devtoberfest Week 2 Preview

  • Devtoberfest Contest Week 2: Coming Soon
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d

SAP TechEd Community Organized Watch Party

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP Community Welcome: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog/session/1723237330354001PDnG
  • SAP Community Watch Zurich: https://community.sap.com/t5/z%C3%BCrich-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-zurich/ec-p/13873047#M1
  • SAP Community Watch Hamburg: https://community.sap.com/t5/hamburg-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-hamburg/ev-p/13875609
  • SAP Community Watch Sao Paulo: https://community.sap.com/t5/s%C3%A3o-paulo-events/sap-teched-virtual-your-sap-community-viewing-party-s%C3%A3o-paulo/ec-p/13875927#M3
  • SAP Inside Track Bengaluru: https://community.sap.com/t5/bengaluru-events/announcing-sap-inside-track-bengaluru-5th-october-2024/ev-p/13855659

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Extensibility Wizard
2:09 Devtoberfest Week 1 Recap
4:03 Devtoberfest Week 2 Preview
6:56 SAP TechEd Community Watch Party

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 26, 2024.

[Daniel and Pepper] For Petoberfest, I'm here with Pepper, and we're here to talk about the Extensibility Wizard, which was released last week and let users create extensions right from within the UI of their SAP app. For the first release, it will work in S/4HANA Cloud and will let you create custom logic, custom fields known as on stack extensions or custom or extension processes known as side-by-side extensions. [Don't go to sleep.]

One of the big benefits is that it's context aware. So for example, if I'm in the sales order application, it will offer me sales order related event triggers to go with my process. There's a little bit of setup involved. So your S/4 HANA Cloud has to know about your SAP BTP and SAP Build installations. That's fairly simple, just involves destinations, trust and communication arrangements. You also have to set up Event Mesh so that your events from S/4 HANA can arrive to process automation.

Luckily, my esteemed colleague Antonio Larediaga has already created two videos to show you very simply how to set this up. One to get your events from S/4 HANA into Event Mesh and then from Event Mesh to Process Automation. The extensibility wizard will undoubtedly be the talk of TechEd and that's why we've set up a Devtoberfest session where Sesh and Kartik can talk at length about how you set this up and how How do you use it? And you will be able to ask them questions. I will put links to all of the videos, the blogs, and the Devtoberfest session in the description.

[Say goodbye.] Bye-bye. 🐶

[Thomas] It seems like just the other day we were starting our planning for this year's Devtoberfest. And here we are, nearly at the end of the first week of the four-week event. My how's time flown. Our team has been super busy both preparing for SAP TechEd virtual, TechEd on tour, as well as delivering these great Devtoberfest sessions to you. At the end of the first week, we've had a really packed week, great attendance. Up over last year, some more people are finding their way to Devtoberfest, which makes us very happy. We've had some excellent sessions across all the topic days.

Keep in mind that Monday is ABAP and CAP day. Tuesday is our day for tooling, Tooling Tuesday, where we're looking at SAP Build, Build Apps, Build Code, Build Process Automation, and how all these tools are bringing innovations for CAP development, low-code, no-code development, and ABAP development as well. Wednesday is our Integration Day. Thursday is MAD, our Machine Learning, AI, and Data Day. And then finally, Friday is both our Front-end Friday, which looks at SAP UI5 and Fiori and other topics related to front-end tools. But Friday is also our Fun Friday, where we have special activities.

Depending upon when you're watching this news item, There may still be time to enter into Petoberfest. Uh, that is our Fun Friday activity for September 27th. Post a picture of your pet in your work environment and, and you can earn some bonus points as well as have fun checking out everybody else's pet pictures as well.

Next Friday, we're going to have another special event. It's going to be our Techtoberfest day on, uh, October the 4th. And on that day, we're going to be asking you to post pictures of your work environment. Show us your laptop set up, your desk set up, whatever cool kind of thing you have set up in your work environment.

Hey, in week two, we will repeat the same daily track structure that you've that you've experienced in this first week. In fact, all four weeks will follow the same track structure, but our Monday is particularly packed with eight individual sessions across CAP and ABAP. We've got some really great things coming in that day. And it's going to be chock full of content. On Tuesday, I'm particularly interested to see the Build Apps, what's coming soon, where they're going to show both new AI innovations and the introduction of SAP UI5 output for Build Apps. Something that I've personally been looking for, for a while. We've got some cool stuff on the integration day around the event architecture and cloud events. We have content on the mad day around generative AI and using CAP with generative AI as well. So a real generative AI kind of day for that particular Thursday. And then finally, for our front end day, we have building elegant applications with Fiori for and Build Code for Android and an iOS Building applications for iOS as well. So we really have a mobile day focused on both Android and iOS

So we've got quite a selection of content for you all throughout the week and keep in mind once we finish week two we'll go directly into TechEd Virtual brought to you live from Germany And then we'll come back with more Devtoberfest, two more weeks of Devtoberfest after TechEd Virtual. And don't forget to enter into the contest. We have activities every day of the week, either by filling out validation tutorials for what you've just saw in your lecture sessions or completing tutorials that go along with them as well. So we've got lots of fun ways for you to earn points towards the big prize that you could win, win, which is, of course, a trip to one of the TechEd in-person events next year. And keep an eye out for TechEd on Tour, where from the ASUG TechConnect event, we're going to be bringing the winners from last year's Devtoberfest. We'll have some great pictures and videos from that event when it takes place in early November. So we look forward to seeing you in all the Devtoberfest and TechEd activities that are coming your way this month.

[Josh] Hey everybody. Are you as excited as I am that it is almost time for SAP TechEd? That's right. October 8th and 9th, SAP TechEd is happening this year. We're going to be coming to you live from Heidelberg, Germany. But if you can't be with us because it's virtual and you still want to talk with others and meet and watch the entire event, you can organize your own watch party. I know that several watch parties have already been organized for the SAP TechEd keynote, which is an hour and a half. It's going to be Michael Amling, Mohammed Alam, several others are going to be putting lots of great information out in this year's keynote. And right after that, for 30 minutes, me and Nora are going to get together and we're going to be hosting a community welcome.

So lots of watch parties are being organized around the world. The SAP community has several websites that I'll put links to in this video so you can see where others are getting together and maybe you can organize your own watch party as well. You can set it up, you can organize it on the SAP community website, and just let others know that you're going to be getting together to watch the Keynote and the Community Welcome and any other parts of SAP TechEd that you want to watch with others. I'll put a link to the one in Zurich, Hamburg, Sao Paulo, and then I think there's going to be one coming from Bengaluru, there's an SAP Inside Track, an SIT event that is happening on October 5th, and if you register for that, I believe you get an invite to the watch party, which is again happening on October 8th, when SAP's TechEd first day is going to go live at 9 a.m. Central European Time. I don't know where you are, so you have to figure out your own times on yourself. That's it, and I can't wait to see you from TechEd.

entry.title: Export and Import Software Collections in a 3-System Landscape for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Image/data in this blog is from SAP internal systems, sample data, or demo systems. Any resemblance to real data is purely coincidental.

Overview:

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the 3-system landscape consists of three core systems and four tenants, each serving a unique role in the development and deployment process:

Development System:

  • Development tenant (for developer extensibility)
  • Customizing tenant (for business configuration and key user extensibility)

Test System:

  • Test tenant (for validation and testing)

Production System:

  • Production tenant (for live, productive usage)

In a 3-system landscape, you have a development system, test system, and production system. Since development and testing activities are separated into two dedicated systems, it is possible to have advanced development projects in this landscape, enabling developer extensibility options. For more information, see System Landscapes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Configuration content in the 3-system landscape is provided via SAP Central Business Configuration, which is connected only to the development system. The content updates and upgrades in all systems are handled differently from the software updates and upgrades

Overview of Software and Content Upgrade | SAP Help Portal

Development System

    • Development Tenant: Developer Extensibility
      • What is Developer Extensibility? Developer extensibility allows developers to create and manage development projects in SAP S/4HANA Cloud using ABAP Development Tools (ADT). Changes made through ADT are recorded in workbench requests.
        • How transport management works in Developer Extensibility:
          • As a developer, you implement custom ABAP code in the development tenant using ABAP Development Tools (ADT). For instance, you may create a custom business object using the ABAP RESTful application programming model.
          • All the changes are recorded on workbench requests, once you have finished the implementation, you can release the developer extensibility transport via ADT (ABAP Development Tools) through the Transport Organizer view in ADT (ABAP Development Tool), For more info See Transport Organizer.  
          • An administrator imports the developer extensibility transport into the test and production tenants using the Import Collection app in SAP Fiori. For more information about your system landscape and change recording, see 3-System Landscape and Transport Management and Change Recording and Transportation. 
    • Customizing Tenant: Business Configuration and Key User Extensibility
      • Business Configuration: Business configuration changes are handled through SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC). This involves scoping, organizational, or country-specific configurations.
        • When you have made your configuration changes, you release the corresponding transport requests by using the app Export Customizing Transports.
        • The SAP content update is merged with your custom configuration content in your SAP Central Business Configuration workspace, and the delta is automatically deployed to the customizing tenant in the development system, and it is – by default – automatically released, this transport request appears as collection in the SAP Fiori app Export Customizing Transports with status “released” under the name “BC Upgrade”
          • How transport management works for Business Configuration.  
            • After completing project scoping in SAP CBC, a transport request is generated when the workspace is deployed to the customizing tenant.
            • The transport request is visible in the Export Customizing Transports app.
            • Take over the ownership of a request with the type of Central Business Configuration by selecting it and selecting Assign To Me. 
            • Note : All Tasks of the request type Central Business Configuration must have the status released. For more info refer Transport Types for Business Configurations and Managing Customizing Requests
      • Key User Extensibility Transport Process: Key user extensibility allows non-technical users to customize applications (e.g., adding fields or modifying UIs) based on business needs.
        • How transport management works for Key User Extensibility:
            • Create a software collection using the Export Software Collection app and assign your extension items to it.
            • Export the collection to transport the extension items.
            • Import the collection into the test tenant via the Import Collection app.For more detailed steps, refer to How to Manage and Export Your Software Collection | SAP Help Portal

Test System

Once all transport requests from the development system (customizing requests, software collections, and workbench requests) are exported needs to import to Test System using the Import Collection App.

  • Customizing Requests: Use the Import Collection app to import released customizing requests from the development system.
  • Developer Extensibility: Release the transport request, workbench request, developer extensibility request from ADT and import it into the test system using the Import Collection app.
  • Key User Extensibility: Open the Import Collection app, select the software collection, and import the extension items into the test tenant.

    Below are the actions that can occur, and which status is the initial status of an action in a collection version, and which status is the result of an action in a collection version.

    • Import -> Imports the collection version into the current system
    • Forward -> Makes the collection version available for import in the production system. This action is only available in the test system
    • Discard -> Discards a collection version so that it can’t be imported into the current system, nor can it be forwarded for import into a subsequent system. A discarded collection version is automatically transported to all subsequent systems so that any dependencies on the current version no longer exist. Key User Extensibility and CBC collection versions can’t be discarded.
    • Preimport -> The import validation has errors due to dependencies. You can decide to skip these errors and import your collection version without dependencies. Key User Extensibility and CBC collection versions can’t be preimported.
    • Set as Imported -> This status is set if the error is resolved locally in the import system, For more info, you can refer Importing a Software Collection | SAP Help Portal
     Procedure to Import the collection to test system
    • Open the Import Collection app.
    • Select Collection type from the Collection Type drop-down.
    • Select the collection you want to import, once testing is completed, and everything works as expected, the collections can be forwarded to the production system for final deployment. In a 3-system landscape, you can choose to transport your software collections automatically. 
      • Transport Automation: - In the Import Settings, choose the software collections you want to maintain the automation settings for. You can also choose to maintain the automation settings for SAP Scheduled Collections: These are collections scheduled by SAP, such as product-relevant updates that are in specific transports to reach the production system. Choose on which days you want the transport automation to be activated. Then, choose the earliest start time for your import. You can determine the number of hours within which the automated import should start. The start of the automated import depends on the system utilization. At the end of the automated import time range, the import process of collections won't end immediately. It'll still finish running the imports but not start new ones from the transport queue. For more info, you can refer the below help document Transport Automation | SAP Help Portal

Production System

After successful testing, the transport requests are forwarded to the production system. The process is as follows:

  • Customizing Requests, Software Collection, and Workbench Requests, CBC requests are forwarded from the test tenant using the Import Collection app.
  • Once forwarded, you can import them into the production tenant through the Import Collection app.

For more details, refer Importing a Software Collection to the P-System.

Conclusion
The transport process in a 3-system landscape for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud ensures a smooth transition of software collections, business configurations, and extensibility changes from the development environment through to the production environment. Understanding the flow of workbench requests, customizing requests, and software collections helps maintain system integrity and ensures that all changes are appropriately tested before going live.

For 3-System Landscape related to Extensibility and Transport Management, you can refer to the following blogs and KBAs:-
